title: Marathon Digital Q2 2025: Record Revenue and Bitcoin Holdings Amid Global Expansion
text: Marathon Digital Holdings reported record second-quarter 2025 revenue of $238.5 million, a 64% increase from the previous year, beating Wall Street expectations. The company reported net income of $808.2 million ($1.84 per share), a dramatic improvement from a $200 million loss in the prior year quarter. Marathon's Bitcoin holdings saw remarkable growth, increasing 170% year-over-year to 49,951 BTC from 18,488 BTC at the end of Q2 2024, positioning the company as the second-largest corporate Bitcoin holder globally. The market value of Bitcoin holdings increased by more than $4.2 billion or 362% year-over-year. The company reported purchased energy costs per Bitcoin of $33,735, which it claims is among the lowest in the sector. The energized hashrate grew to 57.4 exahashes per second (EH/s), an 82% increase from 31.5 EH/s a year ago. The company held over $5 billion in liquid assets as of June 30, 2025.
